description: Camera HW SW Improvement Project — SoC Multimedia & Camera HAL
domain: SoC Camera HAL (Sony IMX789, 4K60 pipeline, HDR TME)

tickets.description: Customer (OEM-A) requires 4K60 video pipeline with sub-100ms glass-to-glass latency for automotive rearview application. Measured from ISP input to display output. This is a P0 contractual requirement — any violation triggers penalty clause.

tickets.summary: HDR10 support in still image capture path
tickets.description: System requirement derived from OEM-A contract. Still image pipeline must support HDR10 tone mapping using multi-frame stacking (3-frame bracket). Output ICC profile must be P3 wide gamut. Applies to both front and rear camera.

Thermal and battery constraint from platform team. Camera subsystem (ISP + sensor + DRAM) must not exceed 2.5W sustained during standard preview use case on a 28nm process node. Violation causes thermal throttling and user-visible frame drops.

tickets.summary: ISP Pipeline Architecture v1.2 — Multi-thread tile processing
tickets.description: Architecture decision: ISP pipeline shall use tile-based parallel processing with 4 hardware threads. Tile size fixed at 256x64 pixels. FIFO depth 8 tiles. This satisfies CAM-001 latency budget by distributing row processing across parallel execution units. Approved by HW Architecture Review Board.

tickets.summary: Memory subsystem: LPDDR5 dual-channel allocation for camera
tickets.description: Architecture decision: Camera subsystem allocated 2x LPDDR5 channels, 12.8 GB/s total bandwidth. Frame buffer layout: ping-pong double buffer in channel 0, histogram/stats in channel 1. Addresses bandwidth bottleneck identified in CAM-001 latency analysis. Budget leaves 15% margin for burst traffic.

tickets.summary: HDR Tone Mapping Engine — dedicated HW block
tickets.description: Architecture decision: Dedicated HDR TME hardware block to avoid ISP pipeline stall during multi-frame stacking. Operates on offline queue with 4-frame deep buffer. Interfaces with ISP via AXI4-stream at 400MHz. Satisfies CAM-002 HDR requirement without impacting preview latency.

tickets.summary: HAL3 CaptureRequest scheduler — ring buffer implementation
tickets.description: SW design spec: HAL3 layer implements a 32-entry ring buffer for CaptureRequests. Each slot holds metadata + 3-frame reference for HDR. Dispatcher thread runs at SCHED_FIFO priority 80. Implements CAM-010 tile-processing architecture. Measured latency overhead: 3.2ms per request at 60fps.

tickets.summary: HAL3 CaptureRequest scheduler — lock-free queue implementation (ALTERNATIVE)
tickets.description: SW design spec (ALTERNATIVE to CAM-020): HAL3 layer uses a lock-free MPSC queue for CaptureRequests instead of ring buffer. Claims lower p99 latency (1.8ms) due to elimination of mutex contention under concurrent access. Also claims to implement CAM-010. NOTE: This directly conflicts with CAM-020 — only one scheduler design can be selected.

tickets.summary: Power management: Dynamic Voltage Frequency Scaling (DVFS) for ISP
tickets.description: SW design spec: ISP DVFS governor reduces ISP clock from 800MHz to 400MHz when frame rate drops below 30fps. Uses Linux cpufreq-like interface. Targets CAM-003 power budget (measured reduction: 0.8W). CAUTION in description: frequency transition introduces 12-18ms pipeline drain period — potential conflict with CAM-001 100ms latency budget during scene transitions.

tickets.summary: Sensor driver: MIPI CSI-2 4-lane configuration for Sony IMX789
tickets.description: HW/SW co-design spec: MIPI CSI-2 running at 2.5 Gbps per lane, 4 lanes total (10 Gbps aggregate), for Sony IMX789 sensor. Pixel format: RAW12. Virtual channel mapping: VC0=RGB, VC1=IR for simultaneous dual-stream capture. D-PHY v2.1 compliant. No parent requirement ticket linked — this was derived directly from hardware selection decision.

Test plan: Inject synthetic 4K60 frames via hardware test pattern generator (TPG), measure ISP-to-display latency with hardware timestamp injection at ISP input and display controller output. Target: <100ms at p95. Measurement tool: custom latency_bench.py with 1ms resolution. 1000-frame test window per configuration. Verifies CAM-001.

tickets.summary: HDR image quality validation — VESA DisplayHDR 1000 compliance
tickets.description: Test plan: Capture 24 Macbeth chart patches under controlled D65 illumination (1000 lux), process through HDR pipeline, compare against DisplayHDR 1000 reference. Metrics: Delta-E2000 < 2.0, peak luminance > 1000 nits, color gamut coverage > 90% P3. Uses Konica Minolta CS-2000 colorimeter. Verifies CAM-002 and CAM-012.

Test plan: Measure full camera subsystem power (ISP + sensor + DRAM controller) via PMIC rail shunt resistors using Monsoon HV Power Monitor. Steady-state 60-second measurement period after 30s warmup. Ambient temperature 25°C. Target: < 2.5W average, < 3.0W peak. Verifies CAM-003.

tickets.summary: BUG: Latency spike 120ms observed during AE convergence
tickets.description: Regression introduced in Sprint-3 build 0.4.2. During Auto Exposure convergence (scene brightness change >2 EV), end-to-end latency spikes to 115-130ms for 3-5 consecutive frames. Exceeds CAM-001 <100ms budget. Root cause under investigation. Suspected interaction: DVFS clock transition from CAM-022 (ISP drops to 400MHz) occurring simultaneously with HAL3 dispatcher flush in CAM-020, causing 2-frame pipeline bubble.

tickets.summary: BUG: HDR TME AXI bus contention with ISP at >4K resolution
tickets.description: HDR Tone Mapping Engine observed saturating AXI4-stream interconnect bus when processing 4K HDR frames simultaneously with ISP 4K preview. Measured memory bandwidth: 14.1 GB/s sustained, exceeding 12.8 GB/s allocation defined in CAM-011. Result: 2-4 frame drops per second in simultaneous HDR+preview scenario. Architecture bandwidth budget insufficient for concurrent operation.

tickets.summary: RISK: No verification plan exists for MIPI CSI-2 multi-virtual-channel
tickets.description: CAM-023 specifies simultaneous RGB+IR capture via MIPI virtual channels VC0/VC1, but no verification ticket has been created to validate this feature end-to-end. The dual-stream virtual channel routing is a complex feature with known interoperability issues across SoC revisions. This unverified assumption is embedded in the HAL design with no test coverage plan. Risk: feature may not work on early silicon.

SW design spec: Unified 3A (Auto-Exposure, Auto-White-Balance, Auto-Focus) control loop running at 30Hz. AE uses 256-zone histogram from ISP stats block. AWB uses per-tile (64x64) color temperature estimate with illuminant detection. AF uses Phase Detection AF from IMX789 embedded PDAF pixels. Control loop output feeds back into ISP registers via UMD/KMD interface at vsync boundary. NOTE: This spec has no parent requirement ticket, no associated architecture block, and no verification plan.

tickets.summary: GDPR compliance: local-only face detection, no cloud upload
tickets.description: Legal/compliance requirement from OEM-A for EU market. Any face detection, face recognition, or biometric processing must execute entirely on-device using the SoC NPU. No facial data or derived biometric features may leave the SoC boundary to cloud services. Applies to all camera pipelines: preview, capture, and video recording. Legal deadline: must be demonstrated before EU type approval submission. NOTE: No architecture decision or software design exists yet implementing this requirement.

pre_computed_edges.reasoning: ISP tile architecture explicitly designed to meet 4K60 <100ms latency requirement
Dual-channel LPDDR5 memory provides bandwidth required by ISP tile architecture
Dedicated HDR TME hardware block implements HDR10 multi-frame stacking requirement
Ring buffer HAL3 scheduler implements the ISP tile-based pipeline architecture
Lock-free queue scheduler is an alternative implementation of CAM-010 tile architecture
DVFS governor directly implements the power budget requirement for 1080p30
Latency benchmark test directly verifies the <100ms end-to-end latency requirement
HDR IQ validation test directly verifies the HDR10 still image capture requirement
HDR IQ test validates the HDR TME hardware block output quality
Power measurement test directly verifies the 2.5W power consumption requirement
Latency spike bug directly violates the <100ms latency requirement
Bug root cause involves HAL3 ring buffer scheduler flush during DVFS transition
Bug occurs when DVFS clock transition (CAM-022) coincides with scheduler operation
AXI bandwidth overflow bug violates the memory subsystem architecture allocation
HDR TME contention bug directly involves the HDR hardware block operation
Risk ticket flags that MIPI virtual channel feature in CAM-023 lacks verification
[INFERRED] DVFS 12-18ms clock transition period can push end-to-end latency above 100ms during AE convergence — described in CAM-022 description but no explicit link created
[INFERRED] AXI bandwidth overflow in HDR TME causes frame drops which violate 4K60 latency contract — cross-domain impact not captured in explicit links
[INFERRED] MIPI CSI-2 4-lane config at 10 Gbps is required to feed 4K60 raw frames into ISP — fundamental dependency for latency requirement
[INFERRED] Latency benchmark inherently validates ISP tile architecture performance, not just the requirement
